mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions and consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or threshold crossing fails to predict return direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline MoM
failure_condition: Joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production relative to its 12-month moving average reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which compresses supply chain bottlenecks and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize in > 55% of instances when industrial production exceeds its moving average, or the signal shows zero predictive power during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent forces central banks to delay monetary easing or initiate tightening, raising inflation expectations and discount rates, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the inflation threshold fails to separate winning and losing months with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue baselines, creating a persistent upward drift in equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple rolling windows, or positive payroll changes coincide with negative equity returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP coinciding with a monthly decline in retail sales confirms a broad-based demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint GDP and retail sales contraction fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in industrial production growth signals improving capacity utilization and margin expansion for industrial firms, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 45% of instances when industrial production accelerates, or the signal loses predictive power during recovery phases (Rank IC < 0.03).
